stories.headline: Google and Blackstone are launching a cloud company built on Google chips
stories.blurb: Alphabet's Google is teaming up with private equity giant Blackstone to create a new AI cloud business that will run on Google's own chips, taking aim at fast-growing competitors like CoreWeave. The joint venture marks Google's latest push to compete in the booming market for AI infrastructure beyond its core cloud division.
stories.why_it_matters: Google is betting it can win cloud customers by offering its custom AI chips through a separate company.
stories.original_headline: Google, Blackstone to Create AI Cloud Firm With In-House Chips

stories.headline: Anthropic raises Claude's usage limits and partners with SpaceX on compute
stories.blurb: Anthropic announced higher usage caps for Claude across all subscription tiers and a new compute partnership with SpaceX. The company didn't specify exact new limits, but existing users will see increased message allowances. The SpaceX deal brings additional infrastructure to support Claude's growing demand.
stories.why_it_matters: More headroom for teams hitting Claude's daily caps, plus infrastructure backing from SpaceX.
stories.original_headline: Higher usage limits for Claude and a compute deal with SpaceX

stories.headline: Meta built an AI that learns your habits and runs your apps for you
stories.blurb: Muse Spark is Meta's new AI agent that watches how you use your phone, learns your routines, and can take actions across apps — like ordering food, booking rides, or managing your calendar — without you opening them. It's designed to become a personal assistant that knows your preferences and handles tasks end-to-end.
stories.why_it_matters: AI is moving from answering questions to actually doing things on your behalf across multiple apps.
stories.original_headline: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence

try_this_prompt.title: The ghost writer test
try_this_prompt.prompt: I just wrote something below. Read it, then write the worst possible version someone could create by skimming mine for thirty seconds—same structure, same length, zero thought. Now show me which of my sentences were already that empty.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before you ship the doc, email, or brief you've been staring at too long.

daily_question: If an AI could run one app on your phone completely autonomously, which app would scare you the most?
